violations.description: The weekly calendar is built with plain divs using CSS grid layout but has no role='grid' or semantic <table> structure. Screen readers cannot convey the relationship between day headers and time slots, making the calendar structure unintelligible.
violations.reference_fix: 8 AM

violations.description: Time slot cells respond to click events to create new events but are plain divs with no tabindex, role='button', or keyboard event handlers. Keyboard users cannot navigate to or activate empty time slots to create events.
violations.reference_fix: <div class="day-col" role="button" tabindex="0" aria-label="Wednesday April 8, 8 AM - click to create event" onkeydown="if(event.key==='Enter')this.click()">

violations.description: Mini calendar date cells are plain divs with no tabindex or keyboard event handlers. Users cannot navigate between dates using arrow keys or select a date using Enter/Space. The navigation arrows are also plain spans, not buttons.
violations.reference_fix: 8

violations.description: Calendar events display a title and time but provide no programmatic context about which day they occur on. A screen reader user encountering 'Team Standup, 8:00-8:30' cannot determine that this event is on Wednesday April 8 without navigating back to find the column header.
violations.reference_fix: <div class="event blue" role="button" aria-label="Team Standup, Wednesday April 8, 8:00 to 8:30 AM, confirmed">

violations.description: Event status (confirmed, tentative, cancelled) is conveyed only through colored dots (green, yellow, red) with no text label, icon alternative, or aria-label. Users who cannot perceive color cannot determine the status of any event.

page_description: A calendar scheduling application with a weekly grid view, mini calendar sidebar, time slots from 8 AM to 2 PM with various events, and status indicators. Issues include the calendar grid lacking table/grid semantics, time slots not being keyboard-interactive, mini calendar dates having no keyboard support, events missing day context, and color-only status indicators.
